contract.name: v2/autocomplete/funding_agency.md
contract.description: Deprecated funding agency autocomplete endpoint returning agency matches for provided search text.

search_text.description: Autocomplete substring used to match funding agencies; comparison is case-insensitive and whitespace-sensitive.
search_text.constraints: required; null, empty string, 0, false, or empty collection values return 400 "Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text"; leading/trailing spaces are not trimmed and can prevent matches; truthy non-string scalars are stringified before matching.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return; defaults to 10 when omitted.
limit.constraints: optional; accepts non-negative integers, numeric strings, floats (floored), and booleans; zero yields an empty results array; non-numeric strings return 400 "Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer"; null, objects, arrays, or negative values trigger a 500 HTML Server Error; large values return the entire match set (observed 513 rows with limit=100000).

results.description: Ordered list of funding agency matches.

id.description: Identifier for the funding agency (observed as integer).

toptier_flag.description: Indicates whether the match represents a toptier agency.

messages.description: Array of warning strings; all observed successes return a single deprecation notice.

messages: This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts.

body.detail: Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer

quirks: `search_text` input is not trimmed; leading or trailing spaces can suppress matches.
Truthy non-string scalars for `search_text` are stringified and searched, while falsy scalars trigger the missing-parameter 400 response.
`limit` accepts numeric strings, floats (floored), and booleans without error.
`limit`=0 is allowed and returns an empty `results` array.
Large `limit` values (e.g., 100000) return the entire match set without pagination metadata.
Successful responses always include the deprecation warning message.
risks: Clients must guard against malformed `limit` values; invalid types or negatives provoke HTML 500 responses.
Whitespace-sensitive `search_text` means callers should trim input to avoid silent empty result sets.
Large limits can return all 513 matches in a single payload, which may impact latency or client processing.
Endpoint is formally deprecated and may be removed or changed without notice.
gaps: Autocomplete ranking and ordering rules are undocumented; observed ordering favors certain agencies but criteria are unknown.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"id":1137,"toptier_flag":true,...}],"messages":["This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts."]}

probes.notes: Default request returned toptier match set with deprecation message.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[],"messages":["This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts."]}

probes.notes: Zero limit accepted and returned empty results.
Negative limit triggers HTML 500 server error.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer"}

probes.notes: Non-numeric limit string rejected with structured client error.
Object limit input results in HTML 500 response.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[],"messages":["This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts."]}

probes.notes: Whitespace-padded search returned no matches, confirming lack of trimming.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"id":1432,...}],"messages":["This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts."]}

probes.notes: Extremely large limit returned all 513 matches without pagination metadata.

mismatches: Docs describe JSON errors, but invalid `limit` values (negative, null, or non-scalar) return 500 HTML error pages.
Docs specify `limit` as a number, yet the service also accepts numeric strings, floats, and booleans.
gaps: Autocomplete ranking and ordering rules are undocumented; observed ordering favors certain agencies but criteria are unknown.
risks: Clients must guard against malformed `limit` values; invalid types or negatives provoke HTML 500 responses.
Whitespace-sensitive `search_text` means callers should trim input to avoid silent empty result sets.
Large limits can return all 513 matches in a single payload, which may impact latency or client processing.
Endpoint is formally deprecated and may be removed or changed without notice.
